skill.name: gws-gmail-triage
skill.description: Gmail: Show unread inbox summary (sender, subject, date).
skill.summary: Quickly scan and prioritize unread Gmail messages with sender, subject, and date information.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 13 potential security issues, all are FALSE POSITIVES. The 'external_commands' patterns appear in markdown code blocks showing command-line usage examples. The 'filesystem' patterns are relative markdown links to other documentation files. The 'weak cryptography' patterns match text in YAML description and markdown table headers, not actual cryptographic implementations. This is a documentation-only skill that provides instructions for using the Google Workspace CLI tool.

content.value_statement: Quickly scan and prioritize unread Gmail messages without opening your browser. This skill provides sender, subject, and date information to help you triage your inbox efficiently.
actual_capabilities: Display unread email summaries with sender, subject, and date
Filter messages using Gmail search queries
Customize the maximum number of messages displayed
Include Gmail label names in output
Export results in JSON format for further processing
Operate in read-only mode without modifying mailbox
limitations: Requires the Google Workspace CLI (gws) to be installed
Requires prior Gmail authentication setup via gws
Only displays message metadata, not full message content
Cannot modify or manage emails (read-only operation)

use_cases.title: Quick Inbox Scan
use_cases.description: Rapidly review new unread emails to identify urgent messages requiring immediate attention
use_cases.target_user: Busy professionals receiving high email volume

use_cases.title: Targeted Email Search
use_cases.description: Find specific unread messages from important senders using Gmail query filters
use_cases.target_user: Executives tracking communications from key contacts

use_cases.title: Batch Email Processing
use_cases.description: Export unread message data to JSON for automated analysis or integration with other tools

prompt_templates.title: Basic Unread Scan
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me my unread Gmail messages
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick overview of new emails in your inbox

prompt_templates.title: Limited Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Show the last 5 unread messages from my boss
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused view of recent messages from a specific sender

prompt_templates.title: Custom Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Find unread messages with the label 'urgent' and show them with labels
prompt_templates.scenario: Targeted search using Gmail filters with label visibility

prompt_templates.title: JSON Export
prompt_templates.prompt: Export unread messages to JSON and extract just the subject lines
prompt_templates.scenario: Programmatic access to message data for further processing

output_examples.input: Show me my unread Gmail messages
output_examples.output: Displays a formatted table with columns for sender, subject, and date for up to 20 unread messages

output_examples.input: Export unread messages to JSON and extract the subject lines
output_examples.output: Returns JSON array of message objects, then filters and displays only the subject fields using jq

best_practices: Start with the basic command without flags to get a quick overview of your unread inbox
Use the --max flag to limit results when you only need to see the most recent messages
Combine with Gmail search queries using --query to filter by sender, date, label, or other criteria
Pipe JSON output to jq or other tools when you need to extract specific fields or process data programmatically
anti_patterns: Do not expect to read full message content—this tool only shows metadata (sender, subject, date)
Do not use for bulk email management—this is a read-only triage tool, not a management interface
Do not forget to run 'gws generate-skills' if you get a 'missing shared skill' error

faq.question: Does this tool modify my Gmail messages or labels?
faq.answer: No. This tool operates in read-only mode and only displays message metadata. It never modifies your mailbox.

faq.question: What do I need before using this skill?
faq.answer: You must have the Google Workspace CLI (gws) installed and have completed Gmail authentication setup. Run 'gws gmail +triage --help' if you need help.

faq.question: Can I search for specific types of messages?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the --query flag with any Gmail search query. For example: '--query from:boss' or '--query is:important'.

faq.question: How do I see more or fewer messages in the results?
faq.answer: Use the --max flag to set the maximum number of messages displayed. The default is 20 messages.

faq.question: Can I export the results to other tools?
faq.answer: Yes. Add '--format json' to output in JSON format, then pipe to jq or other tools for processing.

faq.question: What does the --labels flag do?
faq.answer: It adds a column showing Gmail label names for each message, helping you understand message categorization.
